interface Props { name: string; } export const Greeting: React.FC = ({ name }) =>

Hello {name}

;
reason: Typed functional component with named export
useEffect with proper cleanup function

code: {users.map((u) => <UserCard key={u.id} user={u} />)}
reason: Stable keys using unique IDs
Inline TypeScript types are acceptable for simple props
CSS modules for styling — correct pattern

code:
const [count, setCount] = useState(0);
const doubled = useMemo(() => count * 2, [count]);
reason: Hooks at top level with proper dependencies
Typed list component with stable keys

code:
const ref = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
useEffect(() => { ref.current?.focus(); }, []);
reason: DOM access via ref — correct approach
Class component when functional component is preferred
Hook called inside conditional — violates rules of hooks

code: {items.map((item, index) => <Item key={index} data={item} />)}
reason: Array index as key breaks reconciliation for dynamic lists

code: const MyComp = ({ label }) => <button style={{ color: 'red', margin: '10px' }}>{label}</button>;
reason: Inline styles should use CSS modules or styled-components

code:

Hello

; }
reason: Default export in shared component; named export preferred
Missing clearInterval cleanup causes memory leak

code: document.getElementById('root').innerHTML = 'hi';

code:
const fetchData = async () => { const res = await api.get('/data'); setData(res); };
useEffect(() => { fetchData(); }, []);
reason: Async function in useEffect without abort controller or cleanup

code:
interface Props { children: React.ReactNode; }
export const Layout: React.FC<Props> = ({ children }) => <main>{children}</main>;
reason: Wrapper/layout component with typed children — valid pattern

code:

// data not in deps
reason: Stale closure: data used in memo but missing from dependency array
